classified_facts.label: Games Workshop is a vertically integrated business that designs, manufactures, distributes and sells fantasy miniatures and related products from sci-fi and fantasy universes.
classified_facts.rationale: Core business model description essential to business overview section.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop operates 570 retail stores across 24 countries, with 79% of core sales coming from outside the UK.
classified_facts.rationale: Store network scale and international sales mix is a headline market position fact.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop owns multiple primary Warhammer universes: Warhammer 40,000, Warhammer: The Horus Heresy, Warhammer: The Old World, and Warhammer: Age of Sigmar, with smaller brands including Necromunda, Blood Bowl, and The Lord of the Rings.
classified_facts.rationale: IP portfolio detail supports understanding of brand strength and diversification.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop manufactures all products in Nottingham, employing approximately 330 people in the Warhammer Studio which produces hundreds of new sculpts, illustrations, rules and stories annually.
classified_facts.rationale: Manufacturing location and studio headcount is useful operational colour.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop operates three complementary sales channels: Retail stores, Trade (independent retailers), and Online, with digital content reaching hundreds of thousands of people daily through warhammer-community.com and owned social media.
classified_facts.rationale: Sales channel structure and digital reach is important business model detail.

classified_facts.label: Black Library imprint sold over 4.5 million novels in the last financial period; Citadel Miniatures (plastic miniatures) and Forge World (resin miniatures) are the primary product lines.
classified_facts.rationale: Black Library sales figure adds colour on product diversification.

classified_facts.label: Expansion of Warhammer IP across new media channels including animation, live action, and video games through partnerships with new creative partners, while maintaining protection of the core miniatures business.
classified_facts.rationale: IP expansion into new media is a headline strategic initiative for growth narrative.

classified_facts.label: Continued geographic expansion with ongoing store openings to reach customers worldwide, with strategic focus on maintaining 570-store retail network across 24 countries.
classified_facts.rationale: Store network strategy is supporting detail to geographic expansion theme.

classified_facts.label: Balanced growth across three complementary sales channels (Retail, Trade, Online) with long-term goal of growing all channels in harmony while maintaining more independent stockists than company stores.
classified_facts.rationale: Balanced channel growth strategy is a key strategic priority.

classified_facts.label: Continued investment in IP development and product creation through the Warhammer Studio with over £27 million invested annually in design, sculpting, tooling and new miniature development.
classified_facts.rationale: Ongoing investment in IP/product development supports strategy narrative with quantified spend.

classified_facts.label: We delivered the best financial results in our company's history with record sales in our core business and a bumper year of licensing income, achieving this against a backdrop of major geopolitical uncertainty.
classified_facts.rationale: CEO/Chairman headline statement on record results, likely directly quoted.

classified_facts.label: Our mission is to make the best fantasy miniatures in the world, to engage and inspire our customers, and to sell our products globally for a profit. We intend to do this forever. Our 'forever' ethos is fundamental - we take decisions for the long-term, not to make next week's numbers look good.
classified_facts.rationale: Mission statement reflects long-term culture and management philosophy, useful quote.

classified_facts.label: Our strategy is to grow our business through geographic spread, growing all three complementary channels (Retail, Trade and Online) in harmony, with always more independent accounts than our own stores.
classified_facts.rationale: Restates growth strategy already captured elsewhere.

classified_facts.label: In the short term, we measure our success on our ability to grow sales whilst maintaining our core operating profit margin at current levels.
classified_facts.rationale: Management's margin target commentary is important for performance expectations.

classified_facts.label: We are committed to investing in the Warhammer Studio and tooling at an appropriate level every year to continue to deliver new products and keep customers engaged and excited.
classified_facts.rationale: General commitment statement, repetitive of strategic investment point.

classified_facts.label: We are committed to making it easier than ever for people to discover, engage with and immerse themselves in our IP through new partners and by exploring animation, live action, video games and more, while ensuring we do no harm to our core miniatures business.
classified_facts.rationale: Reiterates IP expansion strategy, some duplication with strategic_items__0.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop sells through own retail stores, independent retailers (trade channel), online web stores, and licensing partnerships. In 2024/25 the company had 8,100 independent retailers in 71 countries, up from 7,200 in 2023/24.
classified_facts.rationale: Trade channel scale and growth in independent retailers is a meaningful KPI.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop manages its business centrally from headquarters in Nottingham. The company operates three factories in Nottingham and main warehouse facilities in Nottingham, Memphis and Sydney, plus third-party warehouses in Tokyo and Shanghai.
classified_facts.rationale: Operational footprint detail on factories and warehouses is useful context.

classified_facts.label: The majority of licensing income is generated by video games sales in North America, the UK and Continental Europe. Games Workshop carefully selects licensees and includes minimum guaranteed payments, performance-based royalties, and ongoing approval processes in licence contracts.
classified_facts.rationale: Licensing contract structure provides insight into revenue quality/risk management.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop has telesales teams based in Memphis, Nottingham and Barcelona for trade sales, with additional small telesales teams in Sydney, Tokyo, Shanghai, Singapore, Hong Kong and Kuala Lumpur, operating in 20 languages.
classified_facts.rationale: Telesales team locations are minor operational detail.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop delivered core business profit before tax of over £200 million from Warhammer product sales for the first time, achieving the best financial results in the company's history.
classified_facts.rationale: First time surpassing £200m core PBT milestone is a headline financial achievement.

classified_facts.label: Licensing delivered increases in sales and profit. Space Marine 2 performed well above expectations. Management notes licensing performance will be very difficult to match next year and is focusing on more Warhammer 40,000 games and seeking partners for Age of Sigmar IP on console, PC and mobile.
classified_facts.rationale: Licensing performance and forward-looking commentary on video game strategy is a key narrative point with risk implications.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop recorded a stock provision charge of £7 million in 2024/25 compared to £6 million in 2023/24. The company follows a principle of selling what it makes with minimal excess stock and minimal stock write-offs.
classified_facts.rationale: Stock provision and inventory discipline is relevant risk/operational context.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop increased its cash buffer from £80 million at May 2024 to £85 million at May 2025. The company sets aside three months' worth of working capital, tax payments and planned capital purchases before determining surplus cash for dividends. Dividends have been paid at record levels.
classified_facts.rationale: Cash buffer policy and dividend approach is important capital allocation detail.

classified_facts.label: In July 2024, Games Workshop launched the 4th edition of Warhammer: Age of Sigmar, including a new 'Spearhead' fast-play version. In September, a new edition of 'Kill Team' launched. The company expanded in-territory translation and marketing teams and added a new team to support expansion into South Korea.
classified_facts.rationale: Product launch details support product strategy narrative.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop delivered year-on-year sales growth in all core established countries with impressive organic growth via the export team globally. Trade channel sales in China were over 20% but behind internal growth targets due to resourcing issues, now fully staffed with investment in senior manager structure.
classified_facts.rationale: Regional sales growth commentary including China resourcing issue is useful color.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop operates two existing manufacturing sites (F1 and F2) and is constructing Factory 4 (F4) with completion targeted for summer 2026.
classified_facts.rationale: Factory 4 construction is a significant forward-looking capacity investment supporting growth strategy.

classified_facts.label: A new paint production facility (F3) was purchased for £2.9 million and refurbished, and additional land was purchased for £2.1 million for future capacity expansion.
classified_facts.rationale: Minor capex detail on capacity expansion, supporting but not headline.

classified_facts.label: North America warehouse (Memphis) added 41 staff to reach 121 headcount, installed additional pallet racking, and added 10 robots to increase picking capacity.
classified_facts.rationale: Operational detail on warehouse staffing/robots, peripheral colour.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop operates 570 retail stores globally: 424 low cost format stores, 143 multi-person operating extended hours stores, and three café format stores.
classified_facts.rationale: Key store count KPI showing retail footprint and format mix, important for business overview.

classified_facts.label: The Warhammer Alliance schools programme has approximately 6,200 active school and library clubs worldwide.
classified_facts.rationale: Niche engagement metric, minor colour.

classified_facts.label: My Warhammer active users reached approximately 735,000 at period end (2023/24: 565,000).
classified_facts.rationale: Digital engagement KPI with YoY growth, useful for analyst on customer traction.

classified_facts.label: Warhammer+ subscription service had approximately 232,000 subscribers at period end (2023/24: 176,000).
classified_facts.rationale: Subscription growth metric relevant to digital strategy and monetization.

classified_facts.label: Email subscriber numbers reached approximately 678,000 at period end (2023/24: 598,000).
classified_facts.rationale: Email subscriber growth, contextual engagement metric.

classified_facts.label: On 10 December 2024, Games Workshop announced a long-term partnership with Amazon for adaptation of Warhammer 40,000 universe into films and television series with associated merchandising rights.
classified_facts.rationale: Major strategic licensing deal with Amazon, headline growth driver for IP monetization.

classified_facts.label: Four new PC/console games were announced in the period: Dawn of War Definition Edition, Space Marine Master Crafted Edition, Boltgun 2, and Dark Heresy, plus one new mobile game Supremacy: Warhammer 40,000.
classified_facts.rationale: New game announcements support licensing pipeline narrative but not headline.

classified_facts.label: Trade outlets increased by approximately 900 accounts to approximately 8,100 total accounts.
classified_facts.rationale: Significant trade channel growth metric supporting distribution strategy.

classified_facts.label: During the period, 30 stores were opened (including relocations) and 8 stores were closed, bringing total store count to 570.
classified_facts.rationale: Store opening/closing detail, contextual for retail expansion.

classified_facts.label: North America retail store count increased from 185 to 201 stores, with the team celebrating opening their 200th Warhammer store.
classified_facts.rationale: Regional store growth colour, supports North America expansion narrative.

classified_facts.label: Stock availability challenges across core IP ranges require ongoing work to meet and beat operational metrics on product availability.
classified_facts.rationale: Supply/stock availability risk, contextual operational risk.

classified_facts.label: The video games licensing market backdrop remains challenging in the short term, requiring caution when forecasting royalty income despite Space Marine 2's success.
classified_facts.rationale: Important caution on licensing/royalty income forecasting given reliance on video game success.

classified_facts.label: The viability and ongoing success of licensing deals is broadly out of the company's control and relies on successful development and delivery by licensing partners.
classified_facts.rationale: Licensing dependency risk, supporting detail.

classified_facts.label: Construction of Factory 4 (F4) is underway with targeted completion summer 2026; construction costs of approximately £8 million expected in next financial year.
classified_facts.rationale: Major capex project (F4) with cost guidance, relevant to capital allocation and capacity strategy.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop is evaluating options to establish a warehouse facility in Europe to build capacity and improve service levels, integrated with Systems Improvement Programme.
classified_facts.rationale: European warehouse expansion initiative, contextual strategic detail.

classified_facts.label: Multi-year Systems Improvement Programme (SIP) continues with global warehouse system launched in Australian warehouse; all Australian teams expected to go live with new systems in H2 2025/26, with project completion targeted for 2028/29.
classified_facts.rationale: IT systems programme, supporting operational strategy detail.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop is actively exploring further console and mobile video game opportunities, including Space Marine 3, following the success of Space Marine 2 in reaching a wider audience.
classified_facts.rationale: Video game pipeline expansion (Space Marine 3) important growth driver.

classified_facts.label: New management positions created for Australia/New Zealand and Japan markets with ambitious growth plans starting April 2025, led by internal candidates with experience in staff development.
classified_facts.rationale: Regional management appointments, minor detail.

classified_facts.label: North America retail team plans to find a new location for a café format store on the east coast, with additional exciting news to be shared in coming year.
classified_facts.rationale: Minor retail expansion plan, low materiality.

classified_facts.label: Online sales decreased by 0.1% on a reported basis, but excluding digital sales, Online sales decreased by 5.2% (£3.6 million). The decline was driven by fewer customers ordering directly from home, though orders picked up in Warhammer stores increased by 15.0% to £9.5 million.
classified_facts.rationale: Online sales decline detail with channel shift to in-store pickup, relevant revenue mix commentary.

classified_facts.label: Direct through Trade (trade account orders on online platform) increased by 10.7% (£1.9 million) to £20.0 million in the period.
classified_facts.rationale: Trade channel online growth detail, supporting revenue mix context.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop opened 30 new stores in the period and invested in customer engagement activities including digital content.
classified_facts.rationale: Repeats store opening figure already covered elsewhere.

classified_facts.label: Average RRP increase on miniatures was 5% in the period, with an average of 4% across all other product lines.
classified_facts.rationale: Pricing/RRP increases directly relevant to revenue and margin drivers.

classified_facts.label: Animation costs relate to production of content for Warhammer+, with amortisation reported in cost of sales.
classified_facts.rationale: Accounting treatment detail, immaterial for memo narrative.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop is a vertically integrated business dependent on key manufacturing and warehousing facilities in Nottingham and Memphis to manufacture and deliver products to customers.
classified_facts.rationale: Describes vertically integrated business model, useful context.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop is the only company of its size making fantasy miniatures and the only one with a global presence.
classified_facts.rationale: Key market positioning statement, headline for competitive advantage.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop plans to open approximately 35 new stores in total across North America, Continental Europe and Asia in 2025/26, including opening its first Warhammer store in South Korea.
classified_facts.rationale: Concrete store expansion plan including new market entry (South Korea), important growth indicator.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop added 184 new roles in the period reported, with many of these recruits in factories and warehouse facilities to scale with activity levels.
classified_facts.rationale: Headcount growth detail, minor operational colour.

classified_facts.label: Since May 2024, 209 staff have decided to cross train and transfer to a new job across the business.
classified_facts.rationale: Internal mobility statistic, minor culture detail.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop expects the majority of incremental growth in 2025/26 to come through sales to independent retailers (the Trade channel), and will continue to open more independent retailer accounts.
classified_facts.rationale: Clear strategic priority on trade channel growth, important for revenue outlook.

classified_facts.label: Failure to protect IP may erode competitive advantage and undermine reputation, negatively impacting financial performance. Risk trend is increasing.
classified_facts.rationale: IP protection risk with increasing trend, material given IP-dependent business model.

classified_facts.label: Cyber attacks could result in reputational damage, regulatory fines, inability to operate, IP leaks and negative financial impact. Risk trend is increasing.
classified_facts.rationale: Cyber security risk with increasing trend, material operational risk.

classified_facts.label: Global supply chain disruption and instability may negatively impact manufacturing and distribution operations and ability to meet demand and fulfil orders. Risk trend is increasing.
classified_facts.rationale: Supply chain disruption risk with increasing trend, relevant given manufacturing concentration.

classified_facts.label: Failure to ensure continuous supply from key manufacturing and warehousing facilities due to climate change effects, physical damage, capacity constraints, or IT systems failure could lead to inability to supply customers. Risk trend is stable.
classified_facts.rationale: Facility disruption risk, contextual but stable trend.

classified_facts.label: Short to medium-term climate risks relate to physical impacts (extreme weather affecting supply chain and manufacturing) and transitional changes (carbon and fossil fuel taxation increasing costs). These risks are not currently assessed as material but are being closely monitored.
classified_facts.rationale: Climate risk noted as not material, low priority. [V2: relevance floored to 3 — matches standard risk-register taxonomy]

classified_facts.label: Staff training and development remains a key priority, with focus on fair wages, lifelong learning, succession planning for board and senior management, and ongoing commitment to diversity and inclusion.
classified_facts.rationale: General HR/ESG priorities, boilerplate-like.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop aims to deliver year-on-year sales growth in every major country and expand customer engagement through My Warhammer platform and physical retail expansion.
classified_facts.rationale: Core growth strategy statement relevant to overall thesis.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop will continue deploying its sales matrix approach to reach new audiences with the Warhammer hobby, offering official retail stores, local currency pricing, translated core ranges, and localized marketing support in countries above sales thresholds.
classified_facts.rationale: Sales matrix approach for international expansion, supporting detail.

classified_facts.label: In licensing, the priority is to fully support delivery of the new media deal with Amazon and to sign a few significant licensing deals to launch successful video games, live action or animation shows.
classified_facts.rationale: Licensing priority tied to Amazon deal, important strategic focus.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop maintains commitment to ethical sourcing, staff wellbeing, diversity and inclusion, and continues to collect and report internally on staff ethnicity with no significant trend changes.
classified_facts.rationale: ESG boilerplate with no notable change, low relevance.

classified_facts.label: After a record year, Games Workshop remains focused on delivering its operational plans and working to overcome obstacles. The company will continue to give itself freedom to make mistakes while constantly working on improvements in product quality and manufacturing innovation.
classified_facts.rationale: General management tone on culture and improvement, contextual.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop is not planning any significant changes to the implementation of its core strategy in 2025/26, remaining commercially curious and inquisitive while prioritizing the six key initiatives designed to deliver further sales growth whilst maintaining core operating profit margin.
classified_facts.rationale: Confirms strategic continuity and margin focus, supporting detail.

classified_facts.label: Games Workshop considers its people as its biggest risk and critical necessity, emphasizing that leaders must understand not only what the company does but why it does it that way. The company recruits from within for all senior roles as far as possible to ensure continuity and mitigate risks.
classified_facts.rationale: Highlights people risk and internal promotion culture, contextual management commentary.

classified_facts.label: The company generates revenue from core operations and licensing, with core revenue of £565.0m and licensing revenue of £52.5m in the 52 weeks ended 1 June 2025.
classified_facts.rationale: Restates revenue split already captured in financial figures, redundant.

classified_facts.label: Operating profit increased from £201.8m in the 53 weeks ended 2 June 2024 to £261.3m in the 52 weeks ended 1 June 2025, representing significant growth in the period.
classified_facts.rationale: Direct YoY operating profit growth commentary, key headline for performance narrative.

classified_facts.label: Licensing revenue grew substantially from £31.0m in the 53 weeks ended 2 June 2024 to £52.5m in the 52 weeks ended 1 June 2025, demonstrating expansion in the licensing segment.
classified_facts.rationale: Licensing revenue growth YoY, supports strategic and financial narrative on licensing segment.

strengths.statement: Games Workshop delivered the best financial results in its history, with Total Revenue rising from £525.7m to £617.5m and Operating Profit rising from £201.8m to £261.3m year-on-year, driven by record core sales and a bumper year of licensing income.

The company is a uniquely positioned, vertically integrated business — the only company of its size making fantasy miniatures with a global presence — supported by in-house manufacturing in Nottingham, giving it strong control over quality, IP and margins.

Trade channel growth was particularly strong, with Trade revenue (constant currency) growing 19.9% to £351.7m, and independent retailer accounts increasing by approximately 900 to about 8,100 in 71 countries, broadening distribution reach at low capital intensity.

Digital engagement metrics are growing strongly, with My Warhammer active users increasing from ~565,000 to ~735,000 and Warhammer+ subscribers increasing from ~176,000 to ~232,000, indicating a strengthening owned digital ecosystem that could reduce reliance on paid marketing over time.

Licensing revenue growth from £31.0m to £52.5m, with 81% of licensing revenue from PC and console games and the recently signed long-term Amazon partnership for film/TV adaptation, provides a high-margin, capital-light complement to the core physical products business (Licensing operating profit of £49.5m implies a very high incremental margin).

Strong balance sheet and cash generation, with Cash Generated from Operations of £311.5m, Net Cash Generated from Operating Activities of £247.4m, and a cash buffer increased from £80m to £85m, support continued high dividend payouts and reinvestment.

risks.statement: Disclosed operational risk: stock availability challenges across core IP ranges and ongoing global supply chain disruption/instability could impair the ability to meet demand and fulfil orders, both flagged with an increasing risk trend.

Disclosed operational/licensing risk: the viability and ongoing success of licensing deals is largely outside Games Workshop's control, dependent on partners' successful development and delivery, adding uncertainty to a fast-growing revenue stream.

Disclosed IT/cyber risk: cyber attacks could cause reputational damage, regulatory fines, operational disruption, IP leaks and negative financial impact, with an increasing risk trend; separately, failure to ensure continuous supply from key manufacturing/warehousing facilities due to IT systems failure (among other causes) could impair the company's ability to supply customers.

Disclosed brand and reputation risk: failure to protect intellectual property may erode competitive advantage and undermine reputation, negatively impacting financial performance, with an increasing risk trend — notable given IP (Warhammer universes) is the core value driver of the business.

Disclosed climate risk: short to medium-term physical risks (extreme weather affecting supply chain/manufacturing) and transitional risks (carbon/fossil fuel taxation increasing costs) are being monitored; while not currently assessed as material, the company's dependence on concentrated manufacturing sites in Nottingham and Memphis increases exposure if conditions change.

Inferred concentration risk: the business is heavily dependent on a small number of manufacturing and warehousing facilities (Nottingham, Memphis), which is not separately broken out as a standalone risk category but is referenced across multiple disclosed risks (supply chain, climate, IT) — a single-region disruption could have an outsized impact on group-wide fulfilment.

Inferred sustainability-of-earnings risk: EPS growth (from 458.8p to 594.9p, approximately +29.6%) has outpaced Operating profit growth (from £201.8m to £261.3m, approximately +29.5%) only marginally in this period, but the mechanism—an outstanding share buyback/reduction in share count implied by Profit Attributable to Owners of £196.1m mapping to a higher per-share EPS figure—means future EPS growth could decouple further from underlying operating growth if buybacks slow or reverse, raising a question over the durability of the per-share growth narrative.

Disclosed risk: management explicitly flags that licensing performance (a major driver of this year's record results) will be very difficult to match next year, and the video games licensing market backdrop remains challenging in the short term, creating downside risk to consensus expectations for FY26.

value_drivers.statement: Licensing income has become a material and highly profitable growth driver, with revenue up from £31.0m to £52.5m and operating profit of £49.5m implying an operating margin of roughly 94%, far above the core business's already strong margin.

Expansion into new media (Amazon film/TV partnership, new PC/console/mobile games including Space Marine 3 exploration) represents a call option on IP monetisation beyond the core miniatures business, with management explicitly protecting the core business from dilution of quality or focus.

Geographic and channel expansion continues to be a core value driver, with Trade revenue (constant currency) growth of 19.9% and plans to open approximately 35 new stores in 2025/26 including entry into South Korea, indicating a long runway for continued volume growth beyond mature UK/core markets (79% of core sales from outside the UK).
Pricing power is evident, with average RRP increases of 5% on miniatures and 4% across other product lines, supporting margin and revenue growth alongside volume gains.

Growing owned digital/community engagement (My Warhammer active users up from ~565,000 to ~735,000; Warhammer+ subscribers up from ~176,000 to ~232,000; email subscribers up from ~598,000 to ~678,000) suggests an increasingly direct, owned-audience relationship that could lower reliance on third-party channels and support lower-cost customer engagement and retention over time, though the facts provided do not disclose a specific paid vs. direct traffic acquisition split.

bull_case.statement: Record financial performance in FY25 (Total Revenue £617.5m, Operating Profit £261.3m) demonstrates the durability and scalability of the core miniatures business plus the emergence of a genuinely additive, high-margin licensing segment.

Diversified, balanced growth across Trade (+19.9%), Retail (+11.3%) and continued core business investment (over £27m annually in the Warhammer Studio) reduces reliance on any single channel and supports management's stated strategy of growing all channels in harmony.

New media optionality (Amazon deal, expanding video game slate) could unlock a step-change in licensing income and brand awareness with limited capital outlay from Games Workshop itself, given licensees bear production costs and minimum guarantees de-risk downside.

Strong balance sheet (Total Equity £280.8m, Cash and Cash Equivalents £132.6m) and robust operating cash generation (£311.5m) provide capacity to fund Factory 4 construction, European warehouse expansion, and continued high dividends without balance sheet strain.

bear_case.statement: Management itself has signalled that licensing performance, the standout driver of FY25's record profit growth, will be very difficult to match in FY26, creating a risk of a growth deceleration or even a licensing revenue decline from this year's elevated base.

EPS grew from 458.8p to 594.9p (approximately +29.6%) while Operating profit grew from £201.8m to £261.3m (approximately +29.5%) — broadly in line this period — but the company's substantial dividend payout (£171.4m paid, at record per-share levels) alongside the difference between Profit Attributable to Owners (£196.1m) and Basic EPS suggests share count reduction is a meaningful lever in the per-share growth story; if buyback activity slows, EPS growth could fall below operating profit growth, undermining the sustainability of the current earnings growth narrative for investors focused on per-share metrics.

Online sales (excluding digital) declined 5.2% (£3.6m), driven by fewer customers ordering directly from home, indicating a structural shift in customer behaviour away from direct online purchase toward in-store pickup and Trade channel ordering, which could pressure the economics of the online/digital infrastructure investment if the trend continues.

The business remains geographically and operationally concentrated in a small number of manufacturing hubs (Nottingham, Memphis), exposing revenue growth ambitions to single-point-of-failure risk from supply chain disruption, IT failure, or climate-related events, even as the store network and Trade base expand globally.
China Trade channel growth, while over 20%, fell behind internal targets due to resourcing issues (now addressed), highlighting execution risk in newer, high-potential growth markets.

diligence_questions.statement: What is the underlying split between direct/organic traffic (My Warhammer app, branded search, direct URL) versus paid acquisition for the Online and Trade digital channels? The provided facts include engagement metrics (My Warhammer users, Warhammer+ subscribers, email subscribers) but do not disclose an explicit paid vs. direct traffic mix, which is needed to assess customer acquisition cost advantages.

What proportion of FY25 EPS growth is attributable to share buybacks/share count reduction versus organic profit growth, and what is the company's planned pace of future buybacks? This is critical to assessing whether EPS growth can continue outpacing operating profit growth sustainably.
What specific mitigation plans exist for the increasing-trend risks around cyber security and IP protection, and has the company experienced any material incidents to date?

What are the specific financial terms (minimum guarantees, royalty rates, duration) of the Amazon partnership and other new licensing deals, given licensing profit contributed materially to FY25 results and management flagged difficulty repeating this performance?

What is driving the decline in direct-to-home online ordering (down 5.2% excluding digital), and is this a permanent structural shift toward in-store/Trade fulfillment, or a temporary effect that could reverse?
What contingency plans exist for Factory 4 construction delays or cost overruns, given the targeted summer 2026 completion and reliance on continued capacity expansion to support growth?
